title: Copilot MCP connector error – “RequestFailureConnector request failed"

posts.author: DMalheiro (Diogo Malheiro)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello all,
Im trying to setup MCP connector with Microsoft Copilot Studio.

I have success setting up the connection. Although, it seems that the connection of the tool to Monday is not being made correctly. I have set the API key and it was ok.

Someone has the same issue or know how to solve?

I followed the steps on the documentation correctly. (even the documentation being poor).

Captura de ecrã 2025-09-22 1559511181×909 69.2 KB

RequestFailureConnector request failed

posts.author: mattdorber (Matt)
posts.cookedHtml: Did you get anywhere with this? Trying to connect CoPilot and Monday.com currently it’s extremely difficult.

posts.author: DMalheiro (Diogo Malheiro)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes Matt, now I have the connection running successfully. Although, it has some limitations, it cannot search in subitems level, for example.

posts.author: mattdorber (Matt)
posts.cookedHtml:
Im interested to know how it works as I havent used an MCP before. ChatGPT seems to be suggested I have to host a server?!

Do you literally just connect it as a data source in CoPilot?

posts.author: AdamBerry (Adam Berry)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Matt, late response here, but I was able to make the agent within Copilot Studio.

Diogo, not sure if you have found a solution, but I found that changing the agent model delivered different results and allowed me to prompt for different things. Worth testing out.
